title: Executive Order 9476—Authorizing the Secretary of the Interior to Take Possession of and Operate the Mines, Collieries, and Preparation Facilities of Certain Coal Companies

flags.justification: Broad seizure and operating authority, meaningfully bounded by the protective requirements in Secs. 2 and 4, identical in structure to EO 9474.
Sec. 5 sets a concrete 60-day cap after productive efficiency is restored.
Single purpose: seizure of the four named coal companies to end labor-disturbance-caused interruptions.
Sec. 2 requires operation 'in accordance with the terms and conditions of employment which are in effect at the time possession thereof is taken' — a concrete status-quo constraint.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
The 60-day cap in Sec. 5 is a genuine, explicit termination trigger.
No judicial-process-blocking or similar oversight-removal language is present.
The 60-day cap and status-quo-employment-terms requirement provide real, checkable constraints.
A routine application of the established wartime coal-seizure template.
Sec. 3's cooperation directive and Sec. 4's explicit preservation of management's own functions are protective, anti-conflict design features, identical in structure to EO 9474.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 1/20 = 5.0%. Structurally identical to EO 9474, issued the same day, covering four additional named coal companies.
